title: Chopper Base JSON v1
description: Bare-minimum flow definition for a tool domain. Defines the files, procs, and stages that constitute the minimal viable flow. At least one of files, procedures, or stages must be present. When stages are used, Chopper can generate run scripts and stack files where stage fields map directly to stack entries such as N (name), J (command), L (legal exit codes), D (dependency graph), I (inputs), and O (outputs).

domain.description: Domain directory name (e.g., my_domain, sta_pt, power)

owner.description: Domain deployment owner or team responsible for this base configuration

_draft.description: Set by scan operation to mark output as machine-generated draft (not yet curated by domain owner). Scan-generated base.json MUST carry _draft=true.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this base flow covers

options.description: Domain-level options for this base configuration

cross_validate.description: Whether to cross-validate F3 output against F1/F2 results

template_script.description: Domain-relative path to a script under the selected domain root. Chopper executes it once at the end of a successful trim run. Must not contain '..' traversal. Python-level validation (V-26) also resolves the real path via Path.resolve() and asserts it stays within the domain root to prevent symlink escape.

include.description: Files or glob patterns to include (domain-relative, forward slashes, no '..', no '//', no absolute paths). Glob patterns: '*' (any chars except /), '?' (one char except /), '**' (any dirs/subdirs). Examples: 'procs/*.tcl', 'rules/**/*.fm.tcl', 'setup.tcl'. Literal paths take precedence over excludes. MUST NOT be empty if files.include is present.

exclude.description: Files or glob patterns to exclude (domain-relative, forward slashes). Supports same glob patterns as include: '*' (any chars except /), '?' (one char except /), '**' (any dirs/subdirs). Excludes prune only the wildcard-expanded include candidates; literal include paths survive even if they match exclude patterns. MUST NOT be empty if files.exclude is present.

include.description: Proc-level includes: file + proc name pairs. MUST NOT be empty if procedures.include is present.

exclude.description: Proc-level excludes used to prune trace-derived proc candidates. Does not remove explicit procedures.include entries. MUST NOT be empty if procedures.exclude is present.

stages.description: Ordered list of stage definitions for F3 run-file generation. Each stage can produce one stack-file node where `name` maps to `N`, `command` maps to `J`, `exit_codes` maps to `L`, and `dependencies` maps to `D`. MUST NOT be empty if stages is present.

file.description: Domain-relative Tcl file path. Must not contain '..' traversal or double slashes.

procs.description: Proc names to include/exclude from this file. MUST NOT be empty — use files.include for whole-file inclusion.

name.description: Unique stage name. Becomes `N <name>` in the generated stack file.

load_from.description: Predecessor stage for data loading inside the generated run_<stage>.tcl, typically via ivar(src_task). This is separate from the stack-file `D` dependency graph line.

dependencies.description: Parent task names for the stack dependency graph. Becomes the `D` line in the generated stack file (for example `D setup`). Use this when the graph dependency should be explicit in the stack output.

exit_codes.description: Legal exit codes for the stage. Becomes the `L` line in the generated stack file (for example `L 0 3 5`).

command.description: Execution command for the stage. Becomes the `J` line in the generated stack file (for example `J -tool run_script -B BLOCK -T stage_name`).

inputs.description: Input artifact markers. Become one or more `I` lines in the generated stack file.

outputs.description: Output artifact markers. Become one or more `O` lines in the generated stack file.

run_mode.description: Execution mode: serial or parallel. Translated into R <value> in the stack file. Since 2025.12.

language.description: Language of the stage. Set to 'python' if the stage uses Python. Default is tcl.

steps.description: Ordered list of step strings that Chopper writes into the generated run_<stage>.tcl or stage script. These are not stack-file lines themselves; they define the body executed by the stage command.
